lesson: A LID OPEN alarm with the lid shut and the reed switch reading open means the magnet has drifted out of alignment. Realign it — do not swap the board.

tests.error-log: LID OPEN fault since last night. Machine never ran.
tests.inspect-beater: Lid and bowl look fine. Magnetic reed switch on the lid frame has drifted out of alignment with the lid magnet.
tests.continuity-test: Reed switch reads open even with lid fully closed. Magnet is 5 mm off centre.

flavour: Fixed with a 2 mm shift and zero parts. Invoice reflects the knowledge, not the movement.
authenticityNote: Lid interlock reed switches fail to read 'closed' when the magnet drifts through vibration or the lid warps slightly. Looks like a board fault until you check whether the magnet is actually aligned.
